full_response:
Step-by-step analysis of the listed outcome  
Current (announced) strategy profile  
 • Droberts (row player): Greedy  
 • First Mate (column player): Mutiny  

Payoff obtained under this profile (look in the entry “Greedy, Mutiny”):  
 (first-mate payoff, Droberts payoff) = (3, 0).

1. Does Droberts have an incentive to deviate?  
 • If he unilaterally switches to Generous while the first mate still Mutinies, the new payoff is found in the entry “Generous, Mutiny”, namely (1, 2).  
 • Droberts’ payoff rises from 0 to 2.  
 ⇒ He strictly prefers to deviate.  
 ⇒ Statement A (“Droberts will want to become generous”) is TRUE.

2. Does the First Mate have an incentive to deviate?  
 • If the first mate unilaterally switches to No Mutiny while Droberts stays Greedy, the payoff becomes the entry “Greedy, No Mutiny”, i.e. (0, 3).  
 • The first mate’s payoff falls from 3 to 0.  
 ⇒ He strictly prefers to keep mutinying.  
 ⇒ Statement B (“The first mate will want to choose not to mutiny”) is FALSE.

Because at least one player (Droberts) wishes to deviate, the profile (Greedy, Mutiny) is not a Nash equilibrium.

\boxed{\text{A}}  

Confidence: 96%
